title: Perses Program Reduction — Syntax-Guided Reduction Framework
summary: A Python-based implementation of syntax-guided program reduction techniques from the Perses family of papers. The framework uses SparTree (syntax-aware parse tree) representation with PNF-normalized grammars to reduce programs while preserving a target property. Five papers are implemented: Perses (base syntax-guided reducer), Vulcan (1-minimality extensions), PPR (pairwise program reduction), LPR (LLM-aided reduction), and Sivand (neural code model interpretability via program reduction). Only Python 3 standard library is required; no external dependencies.
instructionPreview: This project is a Python-based program reduction framework implementing techniques from the Perses family of research papers. Program reduction takes a program that triggers a bug (or exhibits some property) and produces a minimal program that still triggers the same bug.
instruction:
This project is a Python-based program reduction framework implementing techniques from the
Perses family of research papers. Program reduction takes a program that triggers a bug
(or exhibits some property) and produces a minimal program that still triggers the same bug.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The base framework is already present with:
- Token/Tokenizer for Python source
- SparTree (syntax-aware parse tree) with node deletion/cloning
- Grammar in Prioritized Normal Form (PNF) with quantified rules
- Parser that builds SparTree from Python source
- PropertyTester that runs shell test scripts
- DeltaDebugging (ddmin) baseline reducer
- AbstractReducer interface
- ReductionDriver with fixpoint iteration
- Test subjects: bug1 (AttributeError), bug2 (ZeroDivisionError), bug3 (IndexError),
  ppr_bug1 (seed/variant pair with TypeError)

You need to implement all the modules listed below so that all experiments produce
correct results. The experiments are run via `run.sh`.

## Modules to Implement (in order)

1. **Perses** (root paper):
   - `reduction/pnf.py`: PNF normalizer for grammars (unreachable rule elimination,
     single-alternative inlining, recursion elimination, Kleene quantifier introduction)
   - `reduction/perses_reducer.py`: Priority-queue-based syntax-guided reducer using
     ddmin on Kleene children and compatible-descendant replacement on regular nodes
   - Update `run.sh`: DD baseline on bug1, Perses on bug1/bug2/bug3

2. **Vulcan 1-Minimality** (depends on Perses):
   - `reduction/vulcan/__init__.py`: Package init
   - `reduction/vulcan/identifier_replacement.py`: Replace identifiers with shorter names
   - `reduction/vulcan/subtree_replacement.py`: Replace nodes with smaller compatible subtrees
   - `reduction/vulcan/local_exhaustive.py`: Sliding-window exhaustive deletion (window=3, budget=200)
   - Append to `run.sh`: Vulcan experiments on bug1/bug2/bug3

3. **PPR Pairwise Reduction** (depends on Perses):
   - `ppr/__init__.py`, `ppr/diff_utils.py`, `ppr/min_tdiff.py`, `ppr/min_ldiff.py`,
     `ppr/min_commonality.py`, `ppr/driver.py`, `ppr/result.py`
   - Pairwise reduction: Perses(seed) → Perses(variant) → MinTdiff → MinLdiff → MinCommonality
   - Append to `run.sh`: PPR experiment on ppr_bug1

4. **LPR LLM-Aided Reduction** (depends on Perses + Vulcan):
   - `reduction/lpr/__init__.py`, `reduction/lpr/mock_llm.py`, `reduction/lpr/llm_reducer.py`
   - MockLLM provides deterministic regex-based transformations for 5 types
   - 5 concrete LLM reducers extending AbstractReducer
   - Append to `run.sh`: LPR experiments on bug1/bug2/bug3

5. **Sivand Neural Model Interpretability** (depends on Perses):
   - `reduction/sivand/__init__.py`, `reduction/sivand/model_oracle.py`,
     `reduction/sivand/attention_priority.py`, `reduction/sivand/sivand_reducer.py`,
     `reduction/sivand/explanation_finder.py`
   - MockModel for deterministic predictions (keyword-based + fake attention)
   - ModelOracle extending PropertyTester (model prediction = property)
   - AttentionExtractor + AttentionPriorityQueue for attention-guided reduction ordering
   - SivandReducer (Perses-style with model oracle), IterativeAttributor, SaliencyMap
   - ExplanationFinder pipeline orchestrator, FeatureImportanceRanker
   - Append to `run.sh`: Sivand experiment on bug1 reporting SIVAND_* metrics

Only Python 3 standard library is required; no external packages needed.

moduleDag.project: Perses Program Reduction — Syntax-Guided Reduction Framework
moduleDag.description: A Python-based implementation of syntax-guided program reduction techniques from the Perses family of papers. The framework uses SparTree (syntax-aware parse tree) representation with PNF-normalized grammars to reduce programs while preserving a target property. Five papers are implemented: Perses (base syntax-guided reducer), Vulcan (1-minimality extensions), PPR (pairwise program reduction), LPR (LLM-aided reduction), and Sivand (neural code model interpretability via program reduction). Only Python 3 standard library is required; no external dependencies.

nodes.label: Perses Syntax-Guided Reducer
nodes.description: Core syntax-guided program reduction algorithm. Implements PNF normalization for grammars and a priority-queue-based tree traversal that applies ddmin on Kleene children and compatible-descendant replacement on regular nodes. Includes DD baseline for comparison.

nodes.label: Vulcan 1-Minimality Extensions
nodes.description: Three additional reduction strategies: identifier replacement (shorten variable names), subtree replacement (replace nodes with smaller compatible subtrees), and local exhaustive deletion (sliding-window exhaustive pattern deletion with budget).

nodes.label: PPR Pairwise Program Reduction
nodes.description: Pairwise reduction of related programs (seed and variant). Implements MinTdiff (tree-based difference minimization), MinLdiff (list-based ddmin on diff hunks), and MinCommonality (simultaneous common-token deletion). Orchestrated by PPRDriver in a fixpoint loop.

nodes.label: LPR LLM-Aided Reduction
nodes.description: LLM-based source transformations for language-specific reduction. Five transformation types (function inlining, loop unrolling, variable elimination, data type elimination, data type simplification) implemented via MockLLM for deterministic testing. Each transformation is an AbstractReducer that re-parses after applying the transform.

nodes.label: Sivand Neural Code Model Interpretability
nodes.description: Neural code model interpretability via program reduction (Rabin et al., FSE 2022). Repurposes the Perses reduction pipeline for explaining model predictions: a neural model's prediction oracle replaces the compiler/runtime oracle, and the minimal program preserving the prediction reveals essential features. Includes MockModel for deterministic testing, attention-guided priority ordering, iterative attribution, saliency map generation, and an ExplanationFinder pipeline orchestrator.

edges.label: Vulcan reducers complement PersesReducer to push 1-minimality further
PPR uses Perses as the base single-program reducer before pairwise difference minimization
LPR adds LLM transformations to the Perses reduction pipeline
LPR uses Vulcan's identifier/subtree/exhaustive reducers as post-LLM-transform cleanup
Sivand reuses the Perses reduction algorithm with a neural model prediction oracle
